initial_program:
% nonzero cells
nonzero(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% maximum row index (bottom row)
maxRow(MaxRow) :- MaxRow = #max{ R : input(R, _, _) }.

% make MaxRow appear positively elsewhere
maxRowPos(MaxRow) :- maxRow(MaxRow), true.

% count of nonzero cells per column
countNonZero(C, CountC) :- CountC = #count{ R : nonzero(R, C, Color), Color != 0 }, input(0, C, 0).

% make CountC appear positively elsewhere
countPos(C, CountC) :- countNonZero(C, CountC), true.

% rank: number of nonzero rows below R in column C
rank(R, C, Rank) :- Rank = #count{ R2 : nonzero(R2, C, Color2), Color2 != 0, R2 > R }.

% make Rank appear positively elsewhere
rankPos(R, C, Rank) :- rank(R, C, Rank), true.

% row and column predicates (using column0 and row0 as reference)
row(R) :- input(R, 0, 0), color(0).

col(C) :- input(0, C, 0), color(0).

% cells that should be zero (above the block of nonzero cells)
zeroCell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C), maxRowPos(MaxRow), countPos(C, CountC), MaxRow - CountC > R.

% expected output cells: map each original nonzero cell to a bottom row in the same column
forced(R_out, C, Color) :- nonzero(R_in, C, Color), rankPos(R_in, C, Rank), maxRowPos(MaxRow), countPos(C, CountC),
    R_out = MaxRow - Rank, outputCell(R_out, C), row(R_in).

% every cell gets exactly one colour
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- outputCell(R, C).

% zero cells must stay zero
:- zeroCell(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% forced cells must receive the forced colour (unification guarantees this)
:- forced(R, C, Color), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

#show output/3.
